explanation: This command simulates a Windows Installer (msiexec.exe) attempting to install an MSI package with elevated privileges. It includes a typical path under the Windows Installer directory and uses the ALLUSERS=1 flag, which may trigger the Sigma rule.

This command uses the equivalent syntax '-i' instead of '/i' for the install flag and omits quotes around the path. It still represents a valid attempt to install an MSI package with elevated privileges, which may be detected by the Sigma rule.

This command is a straightforward execution of msiexec.exe with the /i flag to install an MSI file. It includes the ALLUSERS=1 flag, which is commonly associated with elevated installation attempts and may be detected by the Sigma rule.

This command uses double backslashes and quotes around the path to simulate a potential bypass technique. It still represents a valid attempt to install an MSI package with elevated privileges, which may be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command uses a standard path and flag format. It is a direct execution of msiexec.exe to install an MSI file with elevated privileges, which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
